title: Compound Interest Calculator
shortDescription: See how an initial amount grows with compound interest over time, including rate, term, and compounding frequency.
metaDescription: Calculate future value, total interest, and growth over time using your principal, rate, term, and compounding frequency.
intro: Enter your principal, annual rate, term, and compounding frequency to see the future value and total interest.
overview:
This calculator shows how money grows when interest is added to the balance and then earns interest again. It is useful for savings plans, deposits, and any situation where the compounding schedule affects the final amount. You can compare different rates, terms, and compounding intervals to see how much the result changes.

The result includes the final balance and the interest earned, so you can separate your own contributions from the growth produced by compounding. If you add regular deposits, make sure they are entered exactly as required by the tool, because a change in frequency or timing can alter the outcome. For loan-style scenarios, a separate payment calculator is usually the better fit.
howItWorks: Enter the starting principal, annual interest rate, time period, and compounding frequency.
The calculator applies compound-interest math to estimate the final balance and total interest.
Review the result, then adjust the inputs to compare different savings scenarios.

useCases.title: Savings plan comparison
useCases.description: Compare two deposit options with the same starting amount but different rates or compounding schedules.

useCases.title: Certificate of deposit estimate
useCases.description: Estimate how a fixed deposit may grow over a set term when interest is credited monthly, quarterly, or yearly.

useCases.title: Goal planning for future balance
useCases.description: Check how much time is needed for a starting balance to reach a target value at a chosen rate.

examples.title: Yearly compounding on a deposit
examples.input: Principal: 8,000; Rate: 4.5%; Time: 6 years; Compounding: yearly
examples.description: Shows how a fixed deposit grows when interest is added once per year.

examples.title: Monthly compounding on a savings balance
examples.input: Principal: 2,500; Rate: 6.2%; Time: 3 years; Compounding: monthly
examples.description: Useful when comparing monthly crediting against a nominal annual rate.

examples.title: High-rate short term scenario
examples.input: Principal: 1,200; Rate: 9%; Time: 18 months; Compounding: quarterly
examples.description: Helpful for checking shorter terms where compounding frequency still matters.

faq.question: What inputs matter most in this calculator?
faq.answer: The starting principal, annual rate, time period, and compounding frequency have the biggest impact on the final value. Even small changes in rate or compounding interval can noticeably change the result.

faq.question: Why does monthly compounding give a different result than yearly compounding?
faq.answer: With more frequent compounding, interest is added to the balance more often, so future calculations are based on a slightly larger amount. That creates a higher final value when the nominal rate stays the same.

faq.question: Does this calculator include taxes, fees, or inflation?
faq.answer: No. It calculates compound growth from the inputs you provide. Taxes, account fees, inflation, and additional deposits or withdrawals need to be considered separately.

faq.question: How should I read the final amount and interest earned?
faq.answer: The final amount is the total balance at the end of the term. Interest earned is the difference between that balance and your starting principal.

faq.question: Why do very small balances sometimes show rounded values?
faq.answer: The result is rounded for display, so tiny decimal differences may not appear. If you need exact accounting values, use the underlying formula with full precision.

seo.title: Compound Interest Calculator
